attributes.readMe:
# GenerateAiImageCommand

## Summary
The single entry point for AI image generation. In one call it runs the raw image engine — builds an OpenRouter chat-completions request (`modalities: ['image','text']`, default model `google/gemini-2.5-flash-image`, the generally-available model that works on the production Vertex provider; the gemini-3.x preview models 404 there unless the project is allowlisted) through `SendRequestViaProxyCommand`, extracts the returned data URL, crops it to the requested aspect ratio (the models ignore the prompt's ratio hint and return a square, so `cropToAspectRatio` enforces the framing deterministically), shrinks it under ~500KB with `compressDataUrl` while preserving the source format, and persists it into the target realm via `WriteBinaryFileCommand` so the result survives the upstream API's ~1h expiry — AND records the result as its own AiImage card (via `SaveCardCommand`) with full provenance: prompt, mode, model, aspect ratio, the `parent` it was derived from, and the inpaint `mask` (itself persisted as a realm file so the record captures WHERE the repaint happened). Routing every generation — studio UI, other card code, or an AI-assistant skill — through this one command makes the generation record a structural guarantee rather than something each call site must remember.

Input (`GenerateAiImageInput`): `prompt`, optional `mode` (inferred when omitted: mask ⇒ inpaint, a parent image ⇒ edit, else generate), `parent` (`linksTo(AiImage)` — the image being iterated on, whose image is the edit source), `maskDataUrl` (inpaint; white = repaint, black = keep), `model`, `aspectRatio`, `targetRealmIdentifier` (required), and `targetPath` (defaults to `generated`). Output (`GenerateAiImageOutput`): the saved `aiImage`, plus `imageUrl` and `contentType` of the persisted file.

## Import
```js
import { GenerateAiImageCommand } from '../commands/generate-ai-image';
```

## Usage
```js
let ctx = this.args.context?.commandContext;
let result = await new GenerateAiImageCommand(ctx).execute({
  prompt: 'a watercolor fox in a misty forest',
  model: 'google/gemini-2.5-flash-image',
  aspectRatio: '16:9',
  targetRealmIdentifier: realmUrl,
  targetPath: 'generated',
});
// result.aiImage is the saved generation record — link it into your history.
```
For a reprompt, pass `parent` (the AiImage being iterated on) so its image becomes the edit source and the new card records it as `parent`; a reprompt may pass a new `aspectRatio`. For an inpaint, also pass `maskDataUrl` and inherit the parent's `aspectRatio` so the repaint lines up with the mask. `mode` can be left unset; it is inferred from those inputs.
